channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Bryan Kohberger doesn't want Amazon shopping list revealed at trial: court filings
item.description: Bryan Kohberger is asking a Boise judge to exclude his Amazon shopping list and 'click activity' from the University of Idaho student murders trial in August.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stock market today: Wall Street's drops kick back into gear
item.description: U.S. stocks were resuming their slide Thursday morning, fueled by concern over AI revenue and Trump's tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Stan Choe, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Second federal judge extends block preventing the Trump administration from freezing funding
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A second federal judge on Thursday extended a block barring the Trump administration from freezing grants and loans potentially totaling trillions of dollars. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump wants to dismantle the Education Department. Here's what it does
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has said he wants his new education chief, Linda McMahon, to “put herself out of a job” and close the Education Department. McMahon was confirmed by the Senate on Monday, and an executive order to shutter the department could come as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Job cuts last month higher than any February since 2009
item.description: U.S.-based employers cut more jobs last month than in any February in 16 years, according to a new report from outplacement firm Challenger, Gray and Christmas.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KREX 5

item.title: Most Greenlanders are Lutheran, 300 years after a missionary brought the faith to the remote island
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — Most Greenlanders are proudly Inuit, having survived and thrived in one of most remote and climatically inhospitable places on Earth. And they’re Lutheran.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Princeton preppy murder suspect had 'fit of madness,' 'terrifying visions' before killing brother: docs
item.description: The Princeton, New Jersey, man accused of killing his brother and a family cat allegedly told police he had a "fit of madness" when they responded to the scene of the crime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Angry Birds, Frogger and others are finalists for the World Video Game Hall of Fame
item.description: ROCHESTER, N.Y. (AP) — This year's finalists for the World Video Game Hall of Fame include trailblazers in esports, electronic pets and portable gaming, as well as the arcade favorite brought to life in a 1998 episode of “Seinfeld.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 5 convicted of plotting to overthrow the German government and kidnap the health minister
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A German court on Thursday convicted five people of plotting to overthrow the government in a far-right coup and kidnap the former health minister. The Koblenz Higher Regional Court sentenced the four ringleaders to prison sentences between five years and nine months to eight years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New Google feature flags when you might be getting scammed in real time
item.description: Google is rolling out new, AI-powered tools to help stop scammers in their tracks, before they get your data or money.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: A professor stands up to Columbia's tolerance of hate
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump expected to sign order calling for Education Department's end
item.description: Trump will sign an executive order on Thursday taking the first steps toward closing the Department of Education, White House officials tell NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A youth theater production rises from the ashes of the Los Angeles fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The day after her house burned down, Lara Ganz sent a group message to the youth theater troupe she runs: They would not let the Los Angeles firestorm stop their upcoming show.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump has dropped a high-profile abortion case in Idaho. Here's what that means
item.description: A yearslong legal battle over the right to an emergency abortion in Idaho has been abruptly upended now that President Donald Trump has moved to drop the high-profile case.
item.dc:creator: Kimberlee Kruesi And Laura Ungar, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former NYPD detective convicted of lying to FBI to protect mafia family
item.description: A New York jury on Wednesday convicted a former detective of lying to authorities in an effort to protect the mafia family whom he moonlighted for.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trudeau not willing to lift Canada's retaliatory tariffs if Trump leaves some tariffs on Canada
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau is unwilling to lift Canada’s retaliatory tariffs on the United States if President Donald Trump leaves any U.S. tariffs on Canada, a senior government official told The Associated Press on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: At least 3 skiers buried in Alaska avalanche, officials say
item.description: An avalanche in the Alaska backcountry swept away and buried three skiers who were believed to be dead under a pile of snow nearly as deep as a 10-story building, Alaska State Troopers said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: By MARK THIESSEN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DOJ announces arrest of men who allegedly smuggled 20K people
item.description: According to the DOJ, the organization illegally moved 20,000 immigrants from 2019 through July 2024.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration deletes list of hundreds of federal buildings targeted for potential sale
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday published a list of more than 440 federal properties it had identified to potentially offload, including the FBI headquarters and the main Department of Justice building, after deeming them “not core to government operations.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans hammer mayors of Boston, Chicago, Denver and New York over 'sanctuary city' policies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican members of Congress hammered four Democratic mayors Wednesday about their so-called sanctuary city policies, accusing them of endangering Americans and threatening to prosecute local officials.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and TIM SULLIVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Baja Midnight, Quesocrisp Taco, and more: What’s coming to Taco Bell in 2025
item.description: Taco Bell announced a slate of new menu items and plans for the fast food chain at its second annual Live Mas event, which took place in New York City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Things to know about avalanches, what causes them and how to stay safe
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Three skiers are believed dead after an avalanche swept them away and buried them under a pile of snow nearly 10 stories deep in the Alaska backcountry. Each winter about 25 people on average die in avalanches in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alleged Abbey Gate plotter extradited to US to face 'justice for our 13,' FBI Director Kash Patel says
item.description: The mastermind behind the Abbey Gate bombing that killed scores of people, including 13 U.S. service members, has been brought back to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans need to cut Medicaid to hit budget savings target, CBO finds
item.description: Republicans can’t achieve their goal of slashing $2 trillion in federal spending over the next decade without cutting Medicaid, according to the nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office (CBO).
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ launches investigation into UC system over antisemitism allegations
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department announced on Wednesday that it has launched a civil investigation into the University of California system under Title VII of the Civil Rights Act. Under Title VII, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump grants one-month exemption for US automakers from new tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is granting a one-month exemption on his stiff new tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada for U.S. automakers, as worries persist that the newly launched trade war could crush domestic manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles County sues Southern California Edison, alleging utility's equipment sparked wildfire
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Los Angeles County said Wednesday that it's suing Southern California Edison, alleging the utility’s equipment sparked January’s Eaton Fire, which destroyed more than 9,400 structures and killed 17 people in the Altadena area.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two Detroit children in a van died from carbon monoxide not excessive cold, autopsies show
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Two Detroit children who recently died in a van in below-freezing temperatures were victims of carbon monoxide, not exposure to cold weather, authorities said Wednesday. Police had said that Darnell Currie Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Student group challenges Texas A&M drag ban
item.description: Texas A&M’s Board of Regents voted almost unanimously late last month in favor of a resolution that states drag events are inconsistent with the system’s “mission and core values.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: All My Friends Are Wrong
item.description: NewsNation Chief Washington Anchor and On Balance host Leland Vittert gives you an early look at tonight’s 7 p.m. ET show.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: As many top Democrats stay silent on Cuomo mayoral run, an accuser feels betrayed
item.description: Lindsey Boylan helped drive Andrew Cuomo from office in 2021 when the former aide came forward as the first woman to accuse the governor of sexual harassment.
item.dc:creator: By Jake Offenhartz And Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Babysitter accused of drowning child during bathtub baptism
item.description: A Muncie woman was babysitting a close friend's children at her home in Texas when she allegedly decided to baptize the children in the tub.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Two men charged after Kansas City Chiefs fans found in backyard
item.description: Two men have been charged in connection to the deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans who were found dead in the backyard of a home in January 2024.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: ICE releases criminal histories of illegal immigrant mother and son arrested in California amid backlash
item.description: ICE is defending itself after it arrested a mother and son pair of illegal immigrants with criminal histories in the United States, the agency said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The US is killing someone by firing squad for the 1st time in 15 years. Here's a look at the history
item.description: It was a punishment for mutiny in colonial times, a way to discourage desertion during the Civil War and a dose of frontier justice in the Old West. In modern times, some consider it a more humane alternative to lethal injection. The firing squad has a long and thorny history in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Magnitude 4.5 earthquake strikes in Washington state
item.description: A “notable” earthquake registering at a preliminary magnitude of 4.5 struck Washington State on Monday morning, according to the United States Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: This rare bean may be your future coffee as warming climate hurts crops
item.description: As leading coffee-producing countries struggle to grow crops in drier, less reliable weather, prices have soared to the highest in decades and the industry is scrambling for solutions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
